mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity risk premiums compress and discount rates fall, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of 2009-07-15 indicates zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window post-2009.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration. When PAYEMS falls by more than 0.5% in a single month, forward equity returns turn negative over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate earnings pressure and recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131,488k as of 2009-07-15 reflects ongoing post-crisis employment losses
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following PAYEMS declines > 0.5%, or the mechanism shows Rank IC < 0.01 over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer and construction demand. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, forward SPX returns are positive over 20 trading days due to improved earnings expectations and reduced recession probability.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 342,309M and HOUST at 582k units as of 2009-06-15 show early recovery signals
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-growth instances, or the interaction effect (measured as joint signal coefficient) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. When INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average, it signals improving supply-side momentum and capacity expansion, predicting positive SPX returns over the next 60 trading days as profit margins expand.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.97 as of 2009-07-15 reflects post-crisis industrial recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of periods when INDPRO is above its 3-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 9% signals severe labor slack and weak wage growth expectations. When UNRATE remains elevated (> 9%), forward SPX returns are negative over 20 trading days because investors anticipate subdued consumer spending and corporate profit pressure.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.4% as of 2009-07-15 indicates persistent post-crisis unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of high-UNRATE periods (> 9%), or the negative direction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate interact to determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL growth is below 2% annualized and FEDFUNDS is near zero, real rates are deeply negative, signaling financial repression that drives equity valuations higher over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 214.47 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of 2009-07-15 indicate deflationary pressure with zero rates
regime_hint: deflationary environment with zero lower bound policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances meeting both trigger conditions, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly signals aggregate demand strength. When GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth after a contraction, forward SPX returns are positive over 60 trading days as the market reprices recession probability downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth following a prior quarter of negative growth
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 12,892.4B (2017 dollars) as of 2009-05-15 reflects early post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of post-contraction recovery quarters, or the positive direction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure supply-side and labor capacity. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, forward SPX returns are positive over 20 trading days because the market interprets dual expansion as sustainable recovery with improving unit labor costs.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and PAYEMS show positive month-over-month growth in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.97 and PAYEMS at 131,488k as of 2009-07-15 show early synchronized recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of co-growth instances, or the interaction effect coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.
